column13: I come from a diverse background , I came to the US at seven years old , it was just me and my mom. We were homeless for a couple months ( felt like years), I got to see my mom struggle working odd jobs and finally getting an apartment with another single mom and her kids and we split the rent on a one bedroom apartment for a couple of years. I learned alot what struggle really meant at an early age. I started finding my purpose over time in helping others and being emphatetic to others. I went to school for Marketing when I graduated it was one of the worst economies in US history in 2009 , i took low marketing roles and at the time I happened to help my mom with her resume and prepped her for her interviews for respitory nurse, at the same time my friend who worked at the airport and had a recent DUI was in need and I helped him with his resume and prepped him for interviews, I then realized I was destined to help people find jobs, I had great intuition and I always see the best in others. Life gave me an opportunity by me applying for a marketing coordinator role via Robert half ( the largest recruitment company world wide at the time). A recruiter by the name of Nadine liked my communication and asked me to come to the office to shadow her and I was beyond excited , it was a perfect match I started in the office team division which focused on receptionist, administrative assistants and warehouse coordinators , I learned so much , I then left to an up and coming agency I learned accoutning and finance skills and started placing direct hire professionals , I became a top producer for this company nationwide and got the opportunity to open up a new market which gave me a different perspective and taught me how to learn a market an execute. After much success I started HR Exchange with my business partner we grew out and had 6 people within our team. After many years I decided to leave our partnership and start fresh with HRX Headhunters , an innovative experience where I work with process improvement oriented companies who I help scale with strategic hiring within their operations including ( Accounting, Finance, Operations, Manufacturing and AI professionals).

Gonzalo’s approach is defined by clarity and candor. He is known for asking the questions others miss, challenging assumptions, and delivering straightforward guidance that helps clients make confident, informed decisions. His ability to bridge rigorous analysis with genuine human understanding sets him apart in an industry often clouded by noise.

Grounded by his Christian values, Gonzalo operates with a strong sense of fairness, honesty, and respect—principles that shape every partnership he builds. He believes that recruitment is not simply about filling positions, but about aligning people with purpose and organizations with the leaders who will define their future.

Today, Gonzalo continues to influence teams, organizations, and careers by connecting exceptional talent with meaningful opportunities. His mission is simple but powerful: help companies grow by placing the right people in the right roles, and do it with integrity, insight, and unwavering commitment.

column19:
1. The “Precision Placement System” (PPS)

A framework for consistently landing high-impact talent.

Step 1 — Role Deconstruction

You break down a role into:

Core success outcomes (not tasks)

Non-negotiable competencies

Failure patterns from previous hires

Hidden expectations the company didn’t articulate

(This alone makes you look smarter than 80% of recruiters.)

Step 2 — Target Persona Building

You craft a candidate persona based on:

experience bandwidth

personality and culture alignment

leadership style

motivations + career trajectory

This eliminates guesswork and shotgun recruiting.

Step 3 — Strategic Sourcing Grid

A systematic approach that maps:

competitor talent

adjacent industries

passive candidate zones

“dark horse” industries clients overlook

This shows clients you’re not fishing… you’re hunting.

Step 4 — Behavioral + Competency Match

You pair evidence-based interviewing with pattern recognition:

decision-making habits

leadership under pressure

conflict resolution style

values alignment

Step 5 — Performance Prediction Review

You deliver “future-focused hiring,” meaning you evaluate:

how they'll perform in 12–24 months

role evolution fit

team synergy

risk flags

It proves you're not just filling a seat — you're shaping the team’s future.

2. The “5C Candidate Evaluation Model”

Fast, clear, and incredibly useful in client conversations.

You evaluate every candidate across:

1. Capability

Can they actually do the job? Skills, track record, scope.

2. Capacity

Do they have the bandwidth for the next level?
Many can do the job today…but not tomorrow.

3. Character

Integrity, accountability, emotional maturity, values.

4. Chemistry

Team fit, leadership compatibility, communication style.

5. Commitment

Are they motivated for this role, this company, this season of life?

This framework makes you sound systematic and deeply thoughtful.

3. The “High-Impact Hiring Blueprint”

Your implementation process for clients.

Phase 1 — Diagnostic

You run a quick organizational x-ray:

talent gaps

leadership dynamics

culture reality vs. culture branding

urgency vs importance

Phase 2 — Hiring Strategy Design

You define:

search strategy

timelines

sourcing channels

screening criteria

decision frameworks

Leaders love this because it gives clarity and control.

Phase 3 — Candidate Acquisition

This is where your headhunting edge shines:

direct outreach

referral mining

competitor mapping

passive talent identification

Phase 4 — Evaluation + Shortlist

You craft a shortlist built on quality, not volume.
Then you provide structured reports:

competency match

risk profile

leadership alignment

predicted performance

Phase 5 — Offer Strategy + Onboarding Success

You guide clients on:

competitive offers

expectation alignment

onboarding roadmap

This elevates you from “recruiter” to talent advisor

column8:
There’s no direct product competitor to Avatar Studio as a whole—we’re the only out-of-the-box “avatar hub” that integrates both HeyGen and Tavus and wraps them in strategy, orchestration, and governance.
The closest players in our space are:

1. HeyGen (Partner + Overlapping Offer)
* What they do well: Enterprise-grade talking-head avatars, strong UX, powerful interactive avatar APIs, fast iteration on features, and solid reliability.
* How we’re different: Avatar Studio sits on top of HeyGen as an enterprise implementation and orchestration layer. We:
    * Design the strategy, use cases, prompts, and workflows.
    * Plug HeyGen into RAG, LLM orchestration, and analytics.
    * Add governance, compliance, and domain specialization (pharma, healthcare, CME, insurance).
    * Provide a multi-avatar, multi-platform environment, not just “one vendor’s avatars.” In short, HeyGen is a core building block; Avatar Studio is the system that makes it a full solution.

2. Tavus (Partner + Overlapping Offer)
* What they do well: Highly scalable personalized and programmatic video, flexible APIs, and strong tooling for outbound, marketing, and dynamic content.
* How we’re different: We integrate Tavus into a broader immersive experience:
    * Combining Tavus with other avatar engines, knowledge bases, and workflows.
    * Using Tavus where personalized or programmatic video makes sense, but always within a governed, strategic architecture. Again, Tavus is a powerful engine; Avatar Studio is the orchestrator that turns that engine into an enterprise-ready, multi-use-case solution.

3. DigitalHumans.com (Direct Competitor, Not a Partner)
* What they do well: High-fidelity 3D digital humans with robust API/SDK options, deep enterprise integrations, and strong backing/partnerships (e.g., Dell). Excellent for premium, cinematic-quality experiences.
* How we’re different:
    * We’re platform-agnostic—we can leverage realistic video avatars, stylized 3D, or a mix of engines depending on use case, budget, and constraints.
    * We focus on speed to value and affordability, offering more accessible paths to pilot and scale than DigitalHumans’ very high price point.
    * Our core strength is AI architecture, governance, and domain expertise, not just the visual layer. We build complete interactive AI systems around the avatar, not just the avatar itself.

Bottom line:
* HeyGen and Tavus are engines we partner with.
* Avatar Studio is the multi-engine cockpit—strategy, design, governance, and orchestration.
* DigitalHumans.com is a premium 3D avatar vendor; we’re the flexible, integrative, and more cost-effective alternative focused on regulated, high-stakes industries.
column11:
I’m the founder and owner of Divigner Group, which I originally launched in the era of traditional web agencies. For years we built websites, portals, and digital experiences—then in 2023 I made a deliberate pivot to transform Divigner into an AI strategy, consulting, and implementation firm. Today, Divigner focuses on AI roadmapping and delivery for insurance, financial services, healthcare, life sciences, and education, often acting as the bridge between business stakeholders, technical teams, and governance.

Alongside Divigner, I co-founded Infiniqo.com in 2017, where we create immersive 3D cyber tabletop exercises and simulation-based experiences that help executives prepare for cyber incidents. I now serve as Chief AI Strategist (previously Chief Product Officer), guiding how we blend AI, game engines, and cyber frameworks to make training memorable, measurable, and aligned with cyber liability and risk management needs.

Before doubling down on entrepreneurship, I spent over two decades in property & casualty insurance, with leadership roles at AIG, Chubb, and Allied World across underwriting, operations, IT, and Insurtech innovation. I’ve supported underwriters in their day-to-day work, built bridges between underwriting, claims, actuarial, finance, and IT, and led AI product initiatives that unify underwriting, claims, reinsurance, and finance data on secure, cloud-native platforms. I’ve overseen teams of AI engineers and DevOps professionals, implemented AI and automation frameworks integrated with policy admin systems like Majesco and EIS, and deployed them on AWS infrastructure. 

Consulting:

More recently, I’ve expanded into life sciences and healthcare, leading an AI-driven portal project for a pharmaceutical company’s Medical Science Liaisons (MSLs) and Healthcare Professionals (HCPs), while continuing AI consulting for both insurance carriers and Main Street businesses. I now bring all of that experience into my current focus: building immersive, interactive AI solutions—from Avatar Studio under Avanew.ai to cyber simulations at Infiniqo—that power insurance training, continuing medical education, and pharma sales rep training. The throughline in my career is simple: I started in web, matured in enterprise insurance and data, and today I specialize in AI-powered, avatar-driven experiences that actually change how people learn, decide, and perform.

Now my mission is to make AI feel human—bridging strategy, creativity, and technology to create real impact.

column4:
I am the Co-Founder of Aligness™, a physician-led advisory integrating surgical medicine, functional medicine, and integrative dental medicine into a systems-based health and performance model for executives and private clients.

Through Aligness, we provide high-touch, personalized health strategy designed to optimize energy, cognition, longevity, and long-term resilience. Our approach moves beyond symptom management and instead identifies root causes across inflammation, metabolic health, airway function, oxidative stress, and systemic load.

In addition to Aligness, I am a surgical ophthalmologist, as well as functional medicine doctor and founder of Bellasee™, where I built an integrative clinical model combining advanced eye surgery with functional medicine principles.

My work centers on redesigning how medicine is delivered — shifting from fragmented care to coordinated, intelligent, performance-driven health systems.
column5:
Over the next 90 days, my goal is to establish Aligness™ as a distinct category in healthcare and position myself as a thought leader at the intersection of integrative medicine, performance strategy, and systems-based health.

I want to significantly increase visibility alongside my co-founder, Dr. Vladimir Pastouk, so that our profiles reinforce one another and clearly communicate our joint model.

Specifically, I want LinkedIn to generate qualified inbound interest from executives, founders, and high-performing professionals who resonate with our approach — leading to strategy calls and private client engagements.

This is not about vanity metrics. The goal is high-caliber visibility that converts into meaningful advisory relationships.
column6:
Our ideal client is a high-performing executive, founder, investor, or professional leader who operates at a high cognitive and decision-making level.

Typical Profile:

CEOs, founders, private equity partners, venture-backed operators

Healthcare leaders, high-level attorneys, entrepreneurs, or professional athletes

Income level: $500K+ annually or significant net worth

Often leading companies with 20–500+ employees

Used to concierge-level service and strategic advisory relationships

What They’re Trying to Solve:

Persistent fatigue, brain fog, or performance plateaus despite “normal” labs

Inflammation-driven health issues (gut, airway, metabolic, autoimmune, ocular surface)

Fragmented care across multiple specialists with no integrated strategy

Desire for longevity optimization and proactive health oversight

Need for private, intelligent, coordinated medical advisory rather than reactive treatment

They are not looking for routine care.
They are looking for clarity, precision, and a systems-based health strategy that matches the level at which they operate professionally.
column7:
I want to be known as the physician building the future of integrated, performance-driven medicine.

When people see my name, they should associate it with systems-based healthcare — connecting surgical insight, functional medicine, and integrative dental medicine into a unified strategy for high-performing individuals.

Not symptom management.
Not fragmented specialties.
But intelligent, coordinated medicine designed for clarity, longevity, and peak cognitive performance.

Aligness™ should become synonymous with that model.
column8:
1. Mark Hyman

What he does well:
Global authority in functional medicine, large media presence, strong thought leadership around root-cause care and longevity.

How we differ:
Aligness™ is not media-driven or mass education–focused. We operate as a high-touch, private advisory integrating surgical medicine, functional medicine, and integrative dental medicine into a coordinated performance model for executives. Our work is deeply personalized and systems-integrated rather than broad public education.

2. Peter Attia

What he does well:
Performance longevity framing, strong credibility in optimization and high-level health metrics, executive audience appeal.

How we differ:
Attia’s model centers on longevity science and individual protocol optimization. Aligness™ integrates multi-disciplinary clinical domains — surgical insight, oral-systemic health, inflammation pathways, airway, ocular surface, metabolic load — into one coordinated strategy. We emphasize systems alignment across specialties, not just protocol design.

3. Cleveland Clinic Executive Health Program

What they do well:
Comprehensive executive physicals, institutional credibility, structured testing programs.

How we differ:
Executive health programs are evaluation-based. Aligness™ is advisory-based and ongoing. We provide strategic oversight, cross-specialty coordination, and longitudinal performance planning rather than episodic testing. Our model integrates dentistry and surgical insight in ways traditional executive health programs do not.
column9:
The one action I want them to take is to book a private strategy call with Aligness™.

Every piece of content should ultimately lead qualified executives and high-performing professionals to request a confidential consultation where we assess their health from a systems perspective and determine whether they are a fit for our advisory model.

The goal is not passive engagement.
It is meaningful inbound conversations that convert into long-term private client relationships.
column10:
Yes.

As a licensed physician, I cannot make public claims that guarantee medical outcomes, promise cures, or imply universal results. All health-related content must remain educational and not constitute individualized medical advice.

I also cannot publicly disclose private client details, case specifics, or outcomes due to confidentiality and, in many cases, NDA agreements.

Additionally, any discussions involving laboratory interpretation, treatment protocols, longevity claims, or performance optimization must remain compliant with medical advertising regulations and avoid unsupported therapeutic claims.

All public-facing content should remain educational, strategic, and thought-leadership oriented rather than prescriptive.
column11:
Please See Current LinkedIn Profile - 25 years of experience in my field.

I am a board-certified ophthalmologist and anterior segment surgeon with extensive experience in corneal disease, endothelial pathology, and advanced microsurgical procedures, including DMEK and complex ocular surface reconstruction.

I am the founder of Bellasee™, an integrative ophthalmology practice where I developed a model combining surgical precision with functional medicine principles to address inflammation, oxidative stress, and systemic contributors to eye disease.

Over the course of my career, I have led clinical operations, built multidisciplinary teams, and integrated advanced diagnostic testing and AI-driven systems into medical practice. My work bridges surgical medicine, systems biology, and performance optimization.

I now serve as Co-Founder of Aligness™, where we are building a physician-led advisory model that integrates ophthalmology, functional medicine, and integrative dental medicine into a coordinated health strategy for executives and high-performing individuals.
column12:
• Board-certified ophthalmologist with over 25 years of clinical and surgical experience, fellowship-trained in cornea and cataract surgery, with advanced expertise in corneal transplantation, anterior segment reconstruction, and complex ocular surface disease.

• Founder and CEO of multiple physician-led ventures, including West Virginia Cornea and Cataract Center of Excellence and Bellasee™ Franchising — building an integrative ophthalmology model that merges surgical precision with functional medicine principles.

• Assistant Professor of Ophthalmology at the Medical University of South Carolina (2008–2011), contributing to residency education and academic development.

• Published in leading peer-reviewed journals including Ophthalmology and Cornea, with research contributions in ocular surface stem cell transplantation, immunologic anterior segment disease, and keratoplasty outcomes. Contributed chapters to major ophthalmology textbooks (Springer, Elsevier).

• Invited national lecturer at The Cornea Society, American Society of Cataract and Refractive Surgery (ASCRS), Bausch + Lomb lecture series, and multiple specialty conferences across the United States.

• Elected member and Vice President of Alpha Omega Alpha Medical Honor Society during medical school.

• Best-selling author of Unshackled: Why Medicine is Failing Doctors (and Patients) – and How We Can Break Free (2025), addressing systemic reform in healthcare delivery.

• Certified Functional Medicine Provider and Medical Cannabis–trained clinician, integrating systems biology, inflammation science, and preventative medicine into surgical practice.

• Licensed in multiple states and active hospital privileges since 2007, maintaining a high-volume surgical practice while simultaneously building scalable healthcare models.
column13:
My defining moment wasn’t in an operating room — it was watching someone I loved move through the healthcare system and realizing how fragmented and reactive it truly is.

When my brother-in-law was diagnosed with cancer, I witnessed firsthand how even intelligent, well-intentioned physicians can operate in silos. There was no unified strategy. No one stepping back to ask why. No coordinated systems thinking. Just protocols.

At the same time, I was building a successful surgical practice — performing advanced corneal transplants, reconstructive procedures, and cataract surgeries — yet I began questioning why so much of what I treated surgically was rooted in inflammation, oxidative stress, and systemic dysfunction that no one was addressing upstream.

That tension changed me.

It led me into functional medicine training. It pushed me to integrate root-cause analysis into surgical care. And ultimately, it inspired me to write Unshackled — a book about how medicine is failing both doctors and patients when it becomes transactional instead of strategic.

My journey has been about breaking out of that model.

Aligness™ was born from that realization — the belief that high-performing individuals deserve coordinated, intelligent, systems-based medical oversight rather than fragmented care.

My story is not about abandoning traditional medicine.
It’s about elevating it.

I SPOKE ABOUT THIS AND MY STORY ON THE MOZA-BELLA MEDIA BROADCAST pinned on my IG channel @bellasee_eyecare; https://www.instagram.com/reel/DMQljjcpQwe/?igsh=MTNoZzFrbGE3eTR4MQ%3D%3D
column14:
What makes me different is that I am not approaching health from a single specialty lens.

I am a fellowship-trained corneal surgeon who moved upstream into systems biology — not because it was trendy, but because I saw inflammation, oxidative stress, and metabolic dysfunction driving the diseases I was operating on.

Most longevity physicians focus on labs.
Most functional medicine doctors focus on root cause.
Most executive health programs focus on testing.

I integrate surgical insight, functional medicine, and integrative dental medicine into one coordinated strategy.

Through Aligness™, we operate on a systems-alignment model:
• Ocular health as a window into systemic inflammation
• Oral and airway health as inflammatory drivers
• Metabolic and mitochondrial performance as cognitive fuel
• Coordinated oversight rather than fragmented specialty care

We don’t just optimize biomarkers.
We map interconnections.

My background allows me to see pathology at the microscopic surgical level while simultaneously evaluating systemic physiology. That dual lens — surgical precision plus systems thinking — is rare.

Aligness™ is not a clinic.
It is a strategic medical advisory built on integration.

column18:
What most of the healthcare and longevity industry gets wrong is fragmentation.

Traditional medicine treats organs separately.
Functional medicine often treats labs separately.
Executive health programs run panels and send reports.

But very few operate from a truly coordinated systems strategy.

We’ve normalized:
• Reactive treatment instead of upstream prevention
• Biomarker optimization without structural context
• Short-term protocols instead of longitudinal oversight
• Siloed specialists with no unifying framework

In ophthalmology specifically, we surgically treat end-stage disease without always addressing the inflammatory and metabolic drivers upstream.

In longevity, we chase metrics without integrating airway health, oral-systemic inflammation, ocular markers, mitochondrial load, and surgical insight into one model.

The mistake isn’t lack of intelligence.
It’s lack of integration.

Medicine has become technically advanced but strategically disconnected.

Aligness™ exists because high-performing individuals don’t need more information — they need coordinated, intelligent oversight.

1. The Fragmentation Problem

Most executives don’t have a health problem.

They have a coordination problem.

One specialist for the heart.
Another for the gut.
Another for hormones.
Another for teeth.
Another for eyes.

No one overseeing the system.

We’ve built advanced medicine — but not integrated medicine.

High performers don’t need more appointments.

They need strategic oversight.

That’s the difference.

2. Longevity Is Not a Lab Panel

Running 80 biomarkers isn’t a longevity strategy.

It’s a data dump.

Longevity isn’t about chasing optimal numbers.

It’s about:

• Inflammation load
• Mitochondrial capacity
• Airway efficiency
• Oral-systemic health
• Cognitive clarity
• Structural resilience

And most importantly — how they interact.

Optimization without integration is noise.

3. Surgery Is Often the End of the Story, Not the Beginning

As a corneal surgeon, I’ve transplanted tissue.

But here’s what medicine doesn’t talk about:

Why did the tissue fail in the first place?

Inflammation.
Metabolic dysfunction.
Oxidative stress.
Autoimmune drivers.

We are exceptional at end-stage intervention.

We are far less disciplined about upstream causation.

That gap is where strategy belongs.

4. Executive Health Is Broken

Executive health programs run impressive tests.

Then they send you home with a PDF.

That’s not advisory.

That’s reporting.

High-level leaders operate with board advisors, financial advisors, strategic consultants.

But when it comes to their health — they get a printout.

Health at the executive level should be longitudinal, coordinated, and strategic.

Not episodic.

5.  Medicine Is Burning Out Its Best Thinkers

Physician burnout isn’t a resilience problem.

It’s a systems design problem.

We trained doctors to think critically.

Then put them in throughput-based models.

Fragmented care hurts patients.

But it also erodes physician purpose.

Reform doesn’t start with more regulation.

It starts with redesign.

Core methodologies include:

1. The Systems Alignment Model™
A multi-domain health mapping process that evaluates inflammation load, metabolic health, airway function, oral-systemic drivers, ocular markers, and cognitive performance as one interconnected system — not separate specialties.

2. The Inflammation Burden Index
A strategic evaluation approach that identifies cumulative inflammatory contributors across oral health, gut health, ocular surface, mitochondrial function, and environmental load to determine root drivers rather than isolated symptoms.

3. Executive Health Oversight Framework
A longitudinal advisory model designed for high-performing individuals, emphasizing coordinated care, strategic lab interpretation, cross-specialty integration, and proactive performance optimization rather than episodic treatment.

4. Surgical-to-Systems Bridge
A clinical philosophy developed from advanced corneal surgery experience — recognizing that end-stage tissue pathology often reflects upstream metabolic and inflammatory dysfunction.

These frameworks allow us to move beyond treatment into strategy.

Aligness™ is not a clinic model.
It is a systems-based advisory model.

column10:
Guarantees of specific clinical outcomes (e.g., “cure,” “100% success,” “permanent fix”).
Promises about treating or curing medical conditions outside scope (e.g., “cures sleep apnea” without appropriate diagnostics/ENT evidence).
Unverified clinical claims or novel therapy efficacy without peer‑reviewed support.
Misstating credentials, board certifications, or fellowship/diploma status.
Implying licensure or certification in states/regions where you are not licensed.
Representing third‑party organizations, endorsements, or partnerships you don’t have.
Advertising prescription or controlled‑substance treatments or instructing patients to stop prescribed therapies.
Making legal, financial, or insurance guarantees (e.g., coverage promises).
Sharing identifiable patient information, photos, or outcomes without explicit, documented written consent (HIPAA compliance).
Using fabricated or manipulated before/after images or misleading photo edits.
Violating manufacturer rules about off‑label device/material promotion.
Disclosing confidential/contractual information (NDAs, unpublished research, proprietary training material).
Medical/public‑health misinformation (e.g., false statements about vaccines, infectious disease treatments).
Soliciting reviews, or offering incentives for positive reviews in jurisdictions where prohibited.

column13: When I was three I found a skull my mother was using for her medical studies and pulled two teeth from the jaw. My grandmother looked at me and said, “You’re a future dentist.” Raised by a physician mother and an engineer father, I grew up between medicine and mechanics — drawn to precise, hands‑on problem solving that improves people’s lives. That childhood curiosity led me from dental school in Kiev to advanced training in the U.S., and eventually to founding the Cleveland Center for Integrative Dentistry. Today I blend surgical precision, biological principles, and functional dentistry (airways, tongue‑tie, ceramic implants, ALF/Myobrace/expanders and clear aligners) to restore breathing, function and confident smiles — a career that began with two teeth and a lifelong desire to fix what’s broken.

column18:
Common mistakes in dentistry (what everyone gets wrong)

Teeth are isolated from overall health — many dentists treat teeth as standalone problems instead of addressing airway, posture, systemic inflammation, and oral‑body connections that drive long‑term outcomes.
Function is secondary to aesthetics — prioritizing quick cosmetic fixes over airway, occlusion, and myofunctional stability leads to relapse and compromised health.
One‑size‑fits‑all implant/restorative approaches — ignoring biocompatibility (metal vs ceramic), immune responses, and individualized biologic protocols.
Underestimating pediatric airway/tongue‑tie impact — delayed diagnosis of frenula or narrow arches misses early windows to prevent lifelong breathing, speech, and orthodontic issues.
Siloed care, poor multidisciplinary collaboration — failure to coordinate with ENT, sleep medicine, myofunctional therapists, and orthodontists reduces treatment effectiveness.
Overreliance on traditional surgery without conservative options — not offering or integrating appliances, myofunctional therapy, or minimally invasive alternatives first.
column19:
Frameworks, processes & proprietary methods
Glossodontics™ — proprietary training program and membership teaching dentists comprehensive dental‑airway functional care (airway assessment, functional orthodontics, myofunctional therapy, frenectomy, and case staging).
Patented frenectomy technique — filed patent for a surgical method for tongue and lip ties (precise, minimally invasive, optimized for faster recovery and better functional outcomes).
Airway Clinical Pathway (Assess → Plan → Treat → Follow‑up) — standardized protocol using sleep/medical history, CBCT airway analysis, interdisciplinary referral (ENT/sleep), custom oral appliance design, and objective follow‑up (sleep testing/myofunctional metrics).
Biological Surgical Protocol — staged, immune‑aware approach integrating biocompatible materials (ceramic implants), ozone therapy/immune‑cell adjuncts, laser/minimally invasive surgery, and guided bone grafting when needed.
Functional Orthodontics Integration Model — coordinated use of ALF, Myobrace, palatal expansion, and clear aligners sequenced with frenectomy and myofunctional therapy to maximize stability and airway improvement.
Clinical Education Model — hands‑on courses + membership follow‑up (case coaching, peer review, and implementation support) to ensure clinicians can replicate outcomes in practice.

column11: Started trading Indian equities at 16 (Zerodha, NSE). Published two research papers by 17. Dropped out of engineering at NTU Singapore at 19 to build Hazlo.ai -- scaled to 2k businesses and exited to SAP. Moved to San Francisco on an Extraordinary Ability visa at 21. Built across the full AI stack: robotics hardware and data (Gerra, $1M ARR), petabyte-scale data infrastructure (Infr, 10K+ MAUs), data brokerage (Lindir, with Brian Norgard). Worked directly with NVIDIA and frontier AI labs. Published a 100K-word thesis at sybilian.com. Proved the thesis with 593% returns in 15 months (2.62 Sharpe, 0.36 beta). Now 24, converting it all into a hedge fund while running multiple companies simultaneously.

column13:
I've been obsessed with understanding how systems work since I was a teenager in India. Started trading at 16, not because someone told me to, but because I wanted to understand why prices move. Published my first research paper at 17 -- a CNN for detecting brain tumors from MRI scans. Dropped out of engineering school in Singapore at 19 because I'd built a company (Hazlo.ai) that was growing faster than anything I was learning in class. Scaled it to $200K ARR and it got acquired by SAP.

Moved to San Francisco on an O-1 visa and started building across the AI stack -- robotics, data infrastructure, GPU clusters. The weird thing was that I kept seeing the same bottleneck from every angle. The robots needed data, the data needed compute, the compute needed energy, and the stocks I was trading were pricing the same constraints I was hitting in my own operations. That's when I realized: the insight isn't in any one layer. It's in seeing all the layers at once.

So I spent a year writing it all down -- 100,000 words, a complete framework for why the economy is reorganizing around AI. Then I traded on it and made 593% in 15 months. Now I'm 24, I'm converting that thesis into a hedge fund, and I'm still building the infrastructure companies that feed the same thesis. Everything compounds into the same thing.
column14:
Everyone else is either a builder OR a trader OR a theorist. I'm all three simultaneously, and they all feed the same thesis. When I say "energy is the bottleneck," it's because I've run the robots that consume the power, built the data pipelines that need the compute, managed the GPU clusters, AND traded the stocks that price the shortage. The thesis isn't theoretical -- it's operational intelligence.

The Sybilian framework itself is the methodology: the economy is a graph, power is compute times connectivity, and for 10,000 years intelligence was roughly symmetric across all nodes. That symmetry is now breaking. Every institution premised on it -- markets, firms, jobs -- is being restructured. The fund identifies where the AI cycle is starved, goes long the bottleneck, and exits when the market prices it in.

column17:
1. What I see operating across the full AI stack -- specific bottlenecks, surprises, and patterns from running robots, managing GPU clusters, brokering data, and trading the equities simultaneously
2. The intelligence symmetry break -- the 10,000-year hidden constant is ending, and what that means for markets, companies, and careers
3. The hyperliquidity -- everything is liquid (capital, talent, attention, capability, information), friction was a filter not just a cost, and direction is the new scarce resource
4. AI supply chain investing -- where the cycle is starved, what's actually bottlenecked vs what people think is bottlenecked, and how I see it from the operator side
5. Building multiple companies at 24 -- not as hustle porn, but honest observations about what works, what doesn't, what's genuinely hard
column18:
- People think AI investing is about picking the best model. It's about identifying where the supply chain is starved -- energy, compute, data -- and going long the bottleneck.
- Everyone talks about "AI replacing jobs" but the real disruption is that intelligence is diverging across nodes for the first time in 10,000 years. Every institution assumes symmetric intelligence. That assumption is breaking.
- The robotics industry is obsessed with model architecture. The real bottleneck is data -- the right data, collected under the right conditions. Always one layer deeper than you expect.
- People treat friction as purely a cost. Friction was also a filter. We removed it, and now we have unlimited options and no direction. That's the real crisis.
- Most content about AI is written by people who read about it. Very few people are simultaneously building AI infrastructure, collecting AI training data, managing the compute, AND trading the public equities. The insight is in the intersection.
column19:
- The Sybilian Framework -- full economic model of the economy as a graph with nodes, edges, and a power function P(n) = C(n) x sum[B(n,i) x P(i)]. Published at sybilian.com.
- The Three Changes -- Hyperliquidity, Information Compression, Zero Activation Energy. Framework for understanding what happens when AI makes everything liquid.
- Bottleneck Investing -- identify where the AI cycle is starved, go long, exit when market prices it in. Proved with 593% returns.
- The Lossy Projection Frame -- assets are lossy projections of reality onto tradeable coordinates. Each new instrument recovers a lost dimension.
- The Hidden Constant -- symmetric intelligence across nodes has held for 10,000 years. It's the hidden variable underlying markets, states, firms, and jobs.
